title: sideritis hirsuta subsp. hirtula
intro: Sideritis hirsuta subsp. hirtula, also known as mountain tea, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Sideritis hirsuta subsp. hirtula include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and potentially lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or supplements that can be consumed for their therapeutic benefits.
